marketState: bearish - seasonal decay and storage surplus outweigh record heat demand
summary: Seasonality is the primary headwind: late-June cooling season carries only a 19% probability of price being higher in four weeks (16-year history), with a typical four-week drift of -1.8% — a deeply bearish setup. Near-term weather is an exceptional counterforce: demand for both the 1-7D and 8-16D windows is above the five-year maximum (+39% vs. 5y normal at 175.3 BCF/d), which explains why the $3.189 front price holds despite bearish fundamentals. Price sits at 73% of the current fair-value band ($2.46–$3.46), yielding a +$0.23 premium to the $2.96 midpoint — elevated but not extreme; fair value compresses the overage toward zero within 21 days as the model lifts to $3.13. Storage at 2,835 Bcf (+151 Bcf above the 5-year average, with a +6 Bcf bearish injection surprise) removes any supply-tightness narrative and caps durable rallies. Managed money is at the 18th percentile of net positioning over five years (net -82,722), marking a crowded short that raises short-squeeze tail risk but does not override the structural bearish bias.

Shorting into an oversold, weather-supported tape carries poor risk-reward; chasing a long flies against the dominant seasonal bias and storage surplus.

keyDrivers: Seasonality: 19% probability higher in 4 weeks, typical 4-week move –1.8% — strongest bearish seasonal signal of the dataset
Extreme weather demand: 1-7D and 8-16D both above 5-year maximum (+39% vs. 5y normal), providing near-term price floor
Storage surplus: +151 Bcf above 5y average with a bearish injection surprise caps rallies and supports seasonal softening
Managed money crowded short (18th percentile, 5y) — short-squeeze tail risk offsets but does not reverse the bearish thesis
Fair-value overage compresses to zero over 21 days as model fair value rises toward $3.13, reducing near-term upside
risks: Extended heat dome through July–August could sustain demand above the 5y max, overriding the seasonal sell signal and lifting price toward the $3.46 band ceiling
